w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Nurture Roots Hour of AI Platform
Nurture Roots Code Club – Hour of AI
Nurture Roots Hour of AI Platform
is a web-based volunteer orchestration and learning coordination platform designed to support a global AI learning programme for children and young people.
Volunteer mentors (primarily Microsoft employees and global professionals)
Learners attending physical community learning hubs in underserved regions
The system automates learner and mentor registration, class allocation, volunteer scheduling, session coordination, impact tracking, and volunteer hours reporting.
The platform will support both online learners and physical hubs while ensuring structured mentor coverage, reliable session delivery, and scalable programme operations.
Nurture Roots Foundation currently runs the
, a weekly global learning initiative focused on introducing children and young people to Artificial Intelligence, coding, and digital literacy.
The programme operates using volunteer mentors and supports learners from diverse global locations, including underserved rural communities through physical learning hubs.
Current coordination activities are largely manual, creating challenges in:
The platform aims to solve these operational challenges and enable global scaling.
To build a scalable global platform that enables:
Efficient coordination of volunteer-led AI education
Seamless support for both remote learners and physical hubs
Ensure reliable mentor coverage for every class
Track volunteer hours and programme impact
Support underserved communities through physical hubs
Enable future scalability across multiple programmes
Children and young people aged 8–18 participating in the programme.
Local representatives responsible for managing physical learning hubs.
The platform shall provide a public landing page containing:
The system shall allow students to register using an online form.
The system shall allow mentors to register.
The system shall allow physical learning hubs to register.
The system shall automatically assign learners into categories based on age.
Classes shall only contain learners from the same category.
The platform shall automatically generate class assignments.
Preference shall be given to grouping hub learners together.
The platform shall generate recurring weekly sessions for approved classes.
The platform shall integrate with Microsoft Teams to:
The platform shall track volunteer hours delivered.
Mentors shall receive reminders after sessions to log volunteer hours.
Mentors shall be able to submit absence requests.
Replacement request is broadcast to all mentors
First eligible mentor to accept is assigned automatically
Emergency absence notifications shall be sent to:
The system shall support automated notifications for:
Notifications shall initially be delivered via email.
The platform shall provide impact metrics including:
The platform will be considered successful if it achieves:
The Nurture Roots Hour of AI Platform will provide the operational infrastructure required to scale a global volunteer-led AI learning initiative efficiently and sustainably.
Enhanced access to AI education globally
while supporting both remote learners and underserved communities through physical learning hubs.
